title: stan.c retail uninitialized locals: 7F0AF20C on-edge flag and 7F0B0914 lastCrossEdge read garbage stack; port zero-inits (quirk not reproduced)
repro: audit: asm_audit.py entries sub_GAME_7F0AF20C + sub_GAME_7F0B0914. (1) lw $s2,0x88($sp) stan.c:1026 with no prior store to sp+0x88 anywhere in the prologue/body (only write is the loop-exit spill at 1177) — s2 is the sticky on-edge flag (bVar2). (2) lw $fp,0x98($sp) stan.c:3463 with no store to sp+0x98 in the function — fp is lastCrossEdge, stored to stanSavedColl_pointI on walk failure (3594)

history.note: Two retail uninitialized-local reads re-derived from GLOBAL_ASM. (Q1) 7F0AF20C: the on-edge seam flag starts as whatever the caller left at sp+0x88; if garbage is nonzero and no tile edge is within 2.0 scaled units, retail still runs the midpoint walk-back disambiguation for candidate tiles (possibly rejecting stacked-floor tiles the zero-init port accepts). Live C zero-inits on_edge (stan.c:746, F2 gate path). (Q2) 7F0B0914: lastCrossEdge starts as garbage from sp+0x98; reachable write-out when the walk fails with every crossing resolving to prevTile/prevPrevTile (fp never assigned) — retail then stores garbage into stanSavedColl_pointI; live C zero-inits (stan.c:3290). Both are deterministic-per-context on console (previous frame contents) but not sanely reproducible on a host port. Disposition: keep zero-init; register in docs/fidelity/QUIRKS.md; treat as known bounded divergence source for movement/seam oracle comparisons (tile pick near seams, stanSavedColl_pointI consumers after failed walks). No fix planned — mitigation IS the zero-init.

Two retail uninitialized-local reads (stan.c 1026 on-edge seam flag at sp+0x88 in sub_GAME_7F0AF20C, stan.c 3463 lastCrossEdge at sp+0x98 in sub_GAME_7F0B0914; no prior store to either offset in the prologue or body) that read garbage stack. On console the value is the deterministic contents left by the previous call or frame; on a host port reproducing it requires reading uninitialized memory (undefined behavior, non-deterministic, possibly crashing) and cannot yield the same bytes as the N64. The port zero-inits both locals (stan.c 746 on-edge flag, stan.c 3290 lastCrossEdge). The mitigation IS the zero-init; the quirk cannot and should not be faithfully replicated. Registered as QUIRKS.md W1.

On console the value is the deterministic contents left by the previous call or frame; on a host port reproducing it requires reading uninitialized memory (undefined behavior, non-deterministic, possibly crashing) and cannot yield the same bytes as the N64. The port zero-inits both locals (stan.c 746 on-edge flag, stan.c 3290 lastCrossEdge). The mitigation IS the zero-init; the quirk cannot and should not be faithfully replicated. Registered as QUIRKS.md W1.
waiver.retest: Re-open if a movement or seam oracle lane shows a tile-pick or stanSavedColl_pointI divergence at scaled tile edges within 2.0 units, which would prove the garbage-dependent branch is reachable and needs an ares or console capture of the actual stack contents to model.
